name: Finland
description: Finland's education system is among the world's best. Top-ranked universities, a strong focus on research and innovation, and a growing number of English-taught programs make Finland increasingly popular.

tuitionFees.postgraduate: Living Expenses of € 9,600 per year to be shown for living expenses + entire duration of the program fees has to be shown in the student’s savings account only https://migri.fi/en/income-requirement-for-students
tuitionFees.phd: Starting January 1, 2025, applicants from non-EU/EEA countries will be required to pay an application fee of EUR 100 when applying for higher education degrees in Finland, excluding licentiate and doctoral degrees.

visaRequirements.processingTime: Finland Student Visa or Residence Permit Expected processing time for electronic application First permit 2 - 3 months Electronic application fee Firs

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 25 hours per week during term; full-time during holidays
workPermit.postStudy: Up to 2 years extended residence permit for job-seeking after graduation

languageRequirements.notes: Many programs taught in English; Finnish required for Finnish-taught programs

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for Finland?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for Finland is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in Finland?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
